---
title: "Article Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle"
author: "Robert C. Cline, CPL"
date: today
---

::: {style="text-align:center; margin-top:2em;"}
![](../images/gtlslogo.png){width="2.0in"}
:::

{{< include ../includes/title-page.qmd >}}

{{< include ../includes/publication-information.qmd >}}

# Executive Summary

*A concise summary (½–1 page) describing the issue investigated, why it matters, the methodology used, principal findings, and practical significance of the paper. This section should usually be written last.*

# Statement of Issues

This paper addresses the following questions:

1.  \[Issue One\]
2.  \[Issue Two\]
3.  \[Issue Three\]

# Scope of Research

Describe what is included in the paper and what is intentionally excluded. This section should identify the legal, technical, administrative, geographic, temporal, and factual boundaries of the research.

# Methodology

Describe the legal authorities, public records, GIS datasets, NRD records, county records, analytical tools, and reproducible methods used in the paper.

# Keywords

\[Keyword One\] • \[Keyword Two\] • \[Keyword Three\] • \[Keyword Four\] • \[Keyword Five\]

\newpage

# Introduction

# Background

# Legal Framework

# Technical Framework

# Analysis

# Discussion

# Conclusions

# Research Opportunities

Describe unanswered questions and future research opportunities.

# References

# Appendices
